source_text: Soft feminine outfits, ballet flats, and wrap sweaters are still showing up heavily in spring fashion trend edits.
People are starting to say balletcore feels overdone compared to newer aesthetics.
Retail roundup shows ballet flats still selling, but trend coverage is less intense than last year.
Neon green visuals and messy party-girl styling are still driving online conversation around brat aesthetic.
Some creators say the brat aesthetic already peaked last summer and is entering parody territory.
Brand campaigns continue referencing brat-coded visuals, suggesting the aesthetic still has cultural reach.
Doechii clips and performance moments are getting shared widely and fans are calling her the next major style and music force.
Music writers are highlighting Doechii's momentum and crossover appeal across fashion and music spaces.
Audience growth remains strong and discussion is still increasing after recent appearances.
Leopard print, oversized fur, and dramatic glam are still circulating, but trend engagement has cooled.
Trend watchers are calling mob wife aesthetic one of the fastest trends to burn out.
Search interest appears to be flattening as users move to softer or more minimal looks.
Collectors are posting unboxings and resale conversations are pushing labubu back into viral territory.
Online demand is rising again as creator content and collector culture make the product feel scarce.
Some users are just discovering labubu, which usually signals room for continued momentum.
